measured_vs_derived.kon_perM_pers: not reported in primary SPR source (null)
measured_vs_derived.koff_pers: not reported in primary SPR source (null)
measured_vs_derived.kint_perday: derived (PD-1 QSP model, bound-receptor internalization kint=kdeg/0.752)

sources.KD_nM: Tan et al. 2017 Nat Commun 8:14369, DOI 10.1038/ncomms14369 (SPR; KD 1.45 nM for WT hPD-1)
sources.fFcRn: IgG4 Fc-competent; FcRn recycling class value
sources.half_life_days: OPDIVO USPI popPK: t1/2=26.7 d (also 25 d single-agent geomean)

nivolumab.notes: IgG4 kappa (S228P hinge-stabilized). KD 1.45 nM is the classic SPR value and matches the prior placeholder. kon/koff not individually reported in the structural SPR paper -> null (derive koff=kon*KD with kon=1e5 if needed). kint/kdeg are anti-PD-1 target values from a dedicated PD-1 QSP model and apply to all four anti-PD-1 mAbs; the model's key finding is that bound-receptor internalization (0.447/day) exceeds free-receptor degradation (0.336/day). Half-life 25 d (single-agent USPI) to 26.7 d (popPK).

sources.KD_nM: Na et al. 2017 Cell Res 27:147-150, DOI 10.1038/cr.2016.77 (apparent KD 27 pM, pembrolizumab Fab/hPD-1)
sources.fFcRn: IgG4 Fc-competent; FcRn recycling class value
sources.half_life_days: KEYTRUDA USPI popPK: t1/2=22 d (older analyses 26 d)

pembrolizumab.notes: IgG4 kappa. KD 27 pM (0.027 nM) from the crystallography paper's reported apparent affinity; matches the prior placeholder. kon/koff not separately reported -> null. kint/kdeg shared anti-PD-1 target values (QSP model). Half-life 22 d (current large popPK) to 26 d (earlier).

measured_vs_derived.kon_perM_pers: not reported in accessible primary source (null)
measured_vs_derived.koff_pers: not reported in accessible primary source (null)

sources.KD_nM: Lu et al. 2022 Front Immunol 13:826045, DOI 10.3389/fimmu.2022.826045 (SPR/Biacore, cemiplimab vs WT PD-1 KD=1.68 nM)
sources.fFcRn: IgG4 Fc-competent; FcRn recycling class value

cemiplimab.notes: REPLACES placeholder (0.6 -> 1.68 nM). 1.68 nM is an independent primary SPR/Biacore value against fully glycosylated PD-1 (N58-glycan-dependent binding). Regeneron's own REGN2810 characterization (Burova 2017 Mol Cancer Ther) was paywalled/403 and could not be fetched; the Lu 2022 value is used. kon/koff not reported in accessible source -> null. IgG4 kappa. kint/kdeg shared anti-PD-1 target values.

sources.fFcRn: IgG4 Fc-competent; FcRn recycling class value

dostarlimab.notes: Complete SPR kinetic set from the preclinical characterization paper: KD=0.3 nM, kon=5.7e5, koff=1.7e-4 (internally consistent: 1.7e-4/5.7e5=2.98e-10=0.30 nM). Matches placeholder. IgG4 humanized. kint/kdeg shared anti-PD-1 target values.

measured_vs_derived.kint_perday: class-estimate (derived from PD-L1 endocytosis/recycling qualitative kinetics)
measured_vs_derived.fFcRn: class-value (see notes: aglycosylation affects FcgR, not FcRn)

sources.KD_nM: Lee et al. 2017 Protein Cell 8:774-777, DOI 10.1007/s13238-017-0412-8 (SPR; atezolizumab KD=1.75 nM)
sources.kint_perday: Burr et al. 2017 Nature 549:101-105 (DOI 10.1038/nature23643) + Cancer Immunol Res 2023;11:866 review (PD-L1 dynamin-dependent endocytosis, ~15-min recycling, ~70% surface at equilibrium)
sources.kdeg_perday: Burr et al. 2017 Nature 549:101-105 (surface PD-L1 degraded within ~6 h absent CMTM6; CMTM6-stabilized otherwise)
sources.fFcRn: TECENTRIQ label (Fc-engineered non-glycosylated IgG1); FcRn binding glycan-independent

atezolizumab.notes: REPLACES placeholder (0.4 -> 1.75 nM). KD_nM=1.75 nM is the MONOVALENT scFv SPR value from Lee 2017 with a complete, self-consistent kon/koff set (1.56e-4/8.93e4=1.75e-9). Alternative: Wang et al. 2021 bioRxiv (DOI 10.1101/2021.08.29.458058) report a bivalent apparent (avidity) KD of 54 pM by SPR (kon 5.7e5, koff 3.1e-5) in buffer for atezolizumab/PD-L1 -- use the 54 pM value if modeling bivalent avidity rather than a monovalent arm. Target is PD-L1 (ligand), not a classical receptor: kint/kdeg are class-estimates flagged as such -- PD-L1 undergoes rapid dynamin-dependent endocytosis with substantial recycling and CMTM6-regulated degradation (surface t1/2 on the order of hours); treat both ~2/day values as order-of-magnitude only. fFcRn: atezolizumab is an Fc-engineered non-glycosylated IgG1 (N297A, EU numbering) -- aglycosylation abolishes FcgR binding/ADCC but NOT FcRn binding (FcRn engages the CH2-CH3 interface independent of the N297 glycan), consistent with its normal ~27-day half-life, so fFcRn=0.89.

measured_vs_derived.kint_perday: class-estimate/derived (from constitutive CTLA-4 endocytosis: majority internalized <5 min)

sources.KD_nM: Ha et al. 2017 Oncotarget 8:60356, DOI 10.18632/oncotarget.18004 (SPR; ipilimumab/CTLA-4 KD=18.2 nM)
sources.kint_perday: Qureshi et al. 2012 JBC 287:9429-9440, DOI 10.1074/jbc.M111.304329 (constitutive clathrin-mediated CTLA-4 endocytosis; majority of surface CTLA-4 internalized within 5 min)
sources.kdeg_perday: Qureshi et al. 2012 JBC 287:9429-9440 (CTLA-4 continuous internalization with recycling+lysosomal degradation)
sources.fFcRn: IgG1 Fc-competent; FcRn recycling class value

ipilimumab.notes: IgG1 kappa. KD=18.2 nM with complete SPR kinetics (ka=3.83e5, kd=6.96e-3; 6.96e-3/3.83e5=1.82e-8=18.2 nM); matches placeholder. NOTE the BMS/FDA IB cites a lower 5.25 nM (average of several studies) and 5.89 nM (tremelimumab) -- the 18.2 nM value has the full published kon/koff and is used here. CTLA-4 is the fastest-cycling target in this panel: kint~200/day is derived from the qualitative finding that the majority of surface CTLA-4 internalizes within ~5 min (rate ~ln2/5min ~200/day) -- order-of-magnitude, flagged class-estimate. kdeg~2/day is a rough class-estimate (CTLA-4 recycles substantially and degrades in lysosomes); treat as order-of-magnitude. Shorter serum half-life (15.4 d) than the anti-PD-1 IgG4s, consistent with high-turnover target-mediated disposition.

measured_vs_derived.KD_nM: measured (SPR, bivalent apparent/avidity; see notes for monovalent)
measured_vs_derived.kint_perday: class-estimate (LAG-3 surface removal dominated by ADAM10/17 shedding, not classical internalization)

sources.KD_nM: Tuli et al. 2022 Cancer Immunol Res 10:1175-1189, DOI 10.1158/2326-6066.CIR-22-0057 (SPR; bivalent apparent KD=0.12 nM at pH 7.4; monovalent Fab KD=10 nM)
sources.kint_perday: Andrews/Vignali LAG-3 reviews (e.g. PMC6920665; PMC9669281): constitutive ADAM10 + TCR-induced ADAM17 cleavage of surface LAG-3
sources.kdeg_perday: Same LAG-3 shedding reviews (constitutive ADAM10 cleavage at D4-TM connecting peptide)
sources.fFcRn: IgG4 Fc-competent; FcRn recycling class value
sources.half_life_days: OPDUALAG PI: relatlimab effective t1/2=26.2 d

relatlimab.notes: IgG4 kappa. KD_nM=0.12 nM is the BIVALENT apparent (avidity) SPR affinity and matches the prior placeholder. IMPORTANT for engager design: the MONOVALENT Fab affinity is 10 nM by SPR (Tuli 2022) -- for a single-armed/monovalent LAG-3 costim or engager arm the 10 nM monovalent value is the physically correct KD; the 0.12 nM only applies to a bivalent IgG binding two LAG-3. kon/koff not reported -> null. LAG-3 surface turnover is dominated by constitutive ADAM10 (+ TCR/PKC-induced ADAM17) ectodomain shedding rather than classical receptor internalization; kint/kdeg here are flagged class-estimates (order-of-magnitude ~1/day). Half-life 26.2 d measured within the OPDUALAG fixed-dose combination.
